strategy_justification: Direct HTTP returns complete server-rendered HTML with all product data (500K+ chars). No JavaScript rendering needed. No anti-bot protection detected. OG meta tags provide structured data (title, price, currency, brand, availability, image, description). CSS selectors provide additional data (sale price, specifications, ratings, review count, SKU, breadcrumbs, images).
proxy_justification: Direct HTTP connection works perfectly (HTTP 200, full page content). No anti-bot blocking observed. No proxy needed.
jsonld_notes: JSON-LD exists but is only BreadcrumbList and VideoObject — no Product-type JSON-LD. Open Graph meta tags compensate fully for the missing Product JSON-LD.
datalayer_notes: serverSideEvents JS variable in the HTML contains rich ecommerce data: SKU, ProductName, Price, Brand, rating (metric2), review_count (metric4), Colors, Sizes, availability, category hierarchy. Extractable via regex from the raw HTML without JS execution.

title.test_result: Eve's Petite Private Pleasure Wand - Vibrators | Adam & Eve
title.note: Page has 2 h1 elements (duplicate in sticky CTA). Use the first one or scope to .ae-product-details h1.ae-h1. The og:title includes a ' - Vibrators | Adam & Eve' suffix — strip it when using as fallback.

price.note: og:price:amount gives the base/regular price. No JS rendering needed.

original_price.note: CRITICAL FIX: Use og:price:standard_amount as PRIMARY source — it's clean and always available on sale products. If not available, use .ae-sale-price .ae-price--was.v1 (note the .v1 modifier — the non-v1 .ae-price--was element contains marketing garbage like '$39.99-$49.99Save Up to $35.02 (71%)'). For the .v1 fallback, the text may still be a price RANGE ('$39.99-$49.99') — extract only the FIRST dollar amount using re.search(r'\$[\d,.]+', text). Do NOT use plain .ae-price--was without .v1 — it includes appended promotional text.

sale_price.note: CRITICAL FIX: For multi-size products, this element contains a PRICE RANGE ('$7.50 - $11.00'), not a single price. The scraper must extract only the FIRST price using re.search(r'\d+\.\d{2}', sale_text) instead of re.sub(r'[^\d.]', '', sale_text) which merges all numbers into one invalid string (e.g., '7.5011.00' causing ValueError crash). Wrap float conversion in try/except.

images.note: CRITICAL FIX: Every product has 2x duplicate images because the carousel has both a thumbnail strip and main display with identical img src values. MUST deduplicate with list(dict.fromkeys(carousel_imgs)) or similar before assigning to product.images.

description.test_result: This petite & powerful wand massager is perfectly sized for your body. Enjoy 10 bed-rocking vibration modes. Order now for discreet shipping & free gift.

sku.note: CRITICAL FIX: The scraper's current regex 'serverSideEvents.*?"Sku":"([A-Z0-9]+)"' fails because the JSON has a space after colon ("Sku": "115H" not "Sku":"115H"). The prefix 'serverSideEvents.*?' with DOTALL causes greedy matching that also breaks. Replace with the simple regex '"Sku":\s*"([A-Z0-9]+)"' which works correctly. The 'Quote item' regex also fails because the SKU is inside a element: 'Quote item E194'. Use 'Quote item[^A-Z0-9]*([A-Z0-9]+)' as secondary fallback, but the Sku regex is primary.

test_result: The perfect size for personal & shared pleasure
Feel 10 functions of powerful vibration, pulsation & escalation
Velvety-smooth silicone head with flexible neck
key_features.note: CRITICAL FIX: The accordion heading is a <summary> element (not <h2>). The scraper uses acc.find('h2', class_=re.compile('ae-accordion__title')) which never matches. Fix: use acc.find(class_='ae-accordion__title') or acc.find('summary', class_=re.compile('ae-accordion__title')). Extract li items from the accordion content div.

specifications.note: CRITICAL FIX: Same <summary> vs <h2> issue as key_features. The accordion heading is a <summary> element. Fix: use acc.find(class_='ae-accordion__title') instead of acc.find('h2', ...). The table class is 'ae-table' and can be found with acc.find('table', class_=re.compile('ae-table')).

page_structure.sku_element: dataLayer serverSideEvents JSON in raw HTML

retry_adjustments.previous_failure_summary: 4/5 products extracted but with 2 HIGH severity issues: (1) original_price contains marketing garbage, (2) sale_price parsing crashes on multi-price text. Plus medium issues with image duplicates and missing key_features/specifications/SKU.

root_cause_analysis.original_price_garbage: The .ae-sale-price container has TWO .ae-price--was spans: the first (without .v1 class) contains appended promotional text ('$59.99Save $47.99 (80%)'), the second (with .v1 class) is clean but may still have price ranges. Solution: use og:price:standard_amount as primary, then .ae-price--was.v1 with regex for first dollar amount.
root_cause_analysis.sale_price_crash: re.sub(r'[^\d.]', '', text) merges multiple prices into one invalid number (e.g., '$7.50 - $11.00' → '7.5011.00'). Solution: use re.search(r'\d+\.\d{2}', text) to extract only the first price.
root_cause_analysis.images_duplicates: Carousel has duplicate img elements (thumbnail strip + main display). Solution: deduplicate with list(dict.fromkeys(list)).
root_cause_analysis.sku_missing: Regex 'serverSideEvents.*?"Sku":"([A-Z0-9]+)"' fails due to greedy DOTALL matching and missing space tolerance. Solution: '"Sku":\s*"([A-Z0-9]+)"'.
root_cause_analysis.key_features_missing: Accordion heading is <summary> not <h2>. Scraper searches for h2.ae-accordion__title which never matches. Solution: use find(class_='ae-accordion__title') or find('summary', class_=...).
root_cause_analysis.specifications_missing: Same <summary> vs <h2> issue as key_features. Solution: same fix.

code_fixes_required.fix: Use og:price:standard_amount as primary source. Fallback: .ae-sale-price .ae-price--was.v1 with regex r'\$[\d,.]+' to extract first dollar amount only.
Replace re.sub(r'[^\d.]', '', sale_text) with sale_match = re.search(r'\d+\.\d{2}', sale_text). Wrap float() in try/except ValueError.
In except block, preserve status_code from the crashed product object instead of hardcoding 0.
Replace regex with '"Sku":\s*"([A-Z0-9]+)"' (no prefix, no greedy DOTALL).
Replace accordion.find('h2', class_=...) with accordion.find(class_='ae-accordion__title') or accordion.find('summary', class_=...). The heading element is <summary>, not <h2>.
